title: Pistachio cake
subtitle: A pale green almond-style cake made with ground pistachios
excerpt: A moist, naturally green cake made with ground pistachios in place of most of the flour: tender crumb, nutty sweetness, and a clean pistachio flavour. Finished with a light mascarpone cream and roughly chopped pistachios.
sourceNotes: Ground-nut cakes using almonds, hazelnuts, and pistachios are a tradition in Mediterranean and Middle Eastern baking. The use of pistachios as the primary flour substitute in a French-style almond cake is a modern adaptation of the classic financier and frangipane methodology. The recipe here follows the basic almond cake formula (common in British baking since the nineteenth century) with pistachios substituted as documented in contemporary British and French patisserie practice.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted cake wrapped tightly. The mascarpone cream does not freeze.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cake keeps well for 2 days at room temperature, unfrosted. Add the cream shortly before serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: Pistachios processed to a fine, slightly moist crumb that replaces most or all of the wheat flour in a nut cake. The natural oils in the ground pistachios keep the cake moist and contribute the green colour and characteristic flavour.

content.text: The colour of this cake comes entirely from the pistachios. Use unsalted, shelled, skinned pistachios for the most vivid green crumb; pistachios with the brown inner skin left on produce a greyer result. Grind them in a food processor to a fine crumb — not a paste, which happens if you over-process. They need
the texture of fine couscous, not pistachio butter.

Preheat the oven to 170°C conventional. Grease and line a 23 cm round tin. Process the pistachios in a food processor until they reach a fine, dry crumb — stop before the oils release. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each. Fold in the ground pistachios, flour, and baking powder in two additions, then the almond extract.

Pour the batter into the prepared tin and smooth the top. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the top is set and a skewer comes out with just a few moist crumbs. The cake will be pale on top. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes, then transfer to a rack. It sinks very slightly in the centre as it cools; this is normal.

Beat the mascarpone and sifted icing sugar together until smooth. Spread over the top of the completely cool cake and scatter the chopped pistachios over the cream. Serve at room temperature; the mascarpone cream softens if the cake is refrigerated for more than an hour.
